title: phreatia parvula
intro: Phreatia parvula, also known as the "Miniature Frangipani," is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, with its roots, leaves, and flowers being used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and skin infections. The health benefits of Phreatia parvula include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
